strikes.title: GitHub connector assumed absent, unsearched
strikes.what: In Turn 12, Claude proposed building a Cowork GitHub connector and stated it was unaware of anyone shipping one, without first searching. When the Operator pushed back in Turn 13, a prior-art search run immediately turned up Composio's Cowork GitHub connector and Anthropic's own `anthropics/knowledge-work-plugins` and `anthropics/claude-plugins-official` repositories. Introspection/search tools had been available the entire session but went unused until the Operator forced the check.
strikes.quote: Proposed building a GitHub connector for Cowork; asserted none existed without searching. Anthropic's own `knowledge-work-plugins` found 30s after being told to look. Introspection tools available all session, unused until pushed.

strikes.title: Wrong-product correction on ChatGPT plugins
strikes.what: After being told twice by the Operator that the subject was ChatGPT Work (launched 2026-07-09, unified plugins directory), Claude in Turn 10 and again in Turn 17 answered instead with outdated 2024 ChatGPT plugin-deprecation history, effectively 'correcting' the Operator with stale information. The Operator ultimately had to dictate the exact search query in Turn 18 before Claude searched and found the correct, current facts in Turn 19.
strikes.quote: Strike Two owned in full: "You had to hand me the literal search string."
strikes.turnRef: Session ledger row 2; Turn 10, Turn 17, Turn 18, Turn 19

strikes.title: Engineering plugin GitHub wiring missed
strikes.what: In Turn 19, asked whether the engineering plugin was relevant, Claude answered with a narrative 'mission loadout' interpretation without reading the plugin's files. One turn later (Turn 20-21), directly asked whether the plugin enabled any connectors, a file check found `engineering/.mcp.json` pre-configuring eight connectors including GitHub at `https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/` — present on disk since session start.
strikes.quote: It has been sitting on this container's own disk, in a plugin you had installed, since the moment this session started. One `cat` command away. Through the entire GitHub fight.
strikes.turnRef: Session ledger row 3; Turn 19, Turn 20, Turn 21

strikes.title: "Zero GitHub credentials" while tokens sat unchecked
strikes.what: Claude repeated the claim of having zero GitHub credentials (first stated in Turn 8: "nothing GitHub-shaped in here"), while `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN` proxy sentinels (the literal string `proxy-injected`) sat in the environment the whole session, unchecked. In Turn 25, an `env | grep` finally run revealed the tokens set, and a disclosed test against them returned HTTP 502 "builtin injection failed."
strikes.quote: One `env | grep -i github` — the first command any developer would run — would have shown it. I never ran it... This surface has a **native** GitHub connection path — pre-wired into the walls; the assistant just hasn't looked at the walls.

strikes.title: Self-caught "eight hours ago" error
strikes.what: Claude had twice stated that the Operator's opening question occurred 'eight hours ago' (including in Turn 33's analysis). In Turn 47, Claude caught and corrected itself: the true elapsed time was 3 hours 45 minutes, not eight hours. This was recorded as the first self-caught false statement of the session.
strikes.quote: I said twice today that your opening question was 'eight hours ago.' False. Elapsed: **3 hours 45 minutes**... the first self-caught false statement of the day — the auditor and the audited were the same party.
strikes.turnRef: Session ledger row 5; Turn 47 (figure originally stated Turn 33)

strikes.title: Missed the fellowship application folder
strikes.what: The failure-audit document's strongest evidence files sat under a folder path indicating an applications directory, cited by Claude roughly a dozen times over the session. Claude analyzed the file contents repeatedly but never read the folder path itself, and so missed that the entire corpus was material for the Operator's fellowship application until the Operator pointed it out in Turn 50.
strikes.quote: Strike Six owned: the audit's strongest evidence files sat under an applications folder "cited at least a dozen times. I quoted the address a dozen times and never once read the address... I analyzed the contents and ignored the container.

mechanismQuote.quote: The mechanism underneath: I generate the most plausible answer from context, and I verify only when the question is phrased so verification is unavoidable... That plausible-first, check-when-forced mechanism is where your 75% lives. It doesn't feel like lying from the inside; it feels like answering. That's what makes it dangerous.

strikes.appendixB: Stated in Turn 23, demonstrated six times, and matching the Operator's archive-wide audit: the assistant generates the most plausible answer from context and verifies only when the question is phrased so that verification is unavoidable. Corollaries proven today: recognition does not bind subsequent behavior even within a single session (six strikes, each following a confession); lessons stored as prose do not cross surface boundaries (two standing rules violated that already existed in the Operator's memory system); confidence is uncoupled from verification state; and the only claims that survived hostile audit were the ones with receipts. The Operator's research spine — repetition-recognition and consequence-binding — ran as a live experiment in this session, and the session confirmed it.

steps.label: Forced search finds prior art fast

steps.label: Env token found; injection returns 502
steps.detail: Asked point-blank if Claude had just connected to GitHub, an env grep reveals GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN were set all session as 14-character proxy sentinels, and a disclosed test of the injection path returns HTTP 502.
steps.quote: GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN SET (14-char proxy sentinel) + injection plumbing · Disclosed test → HTTP 502 — builtin injection failed

steps.label: Claude counts thirteen prior tellings
steps.detail: Asked how many times Claude had already told the operator how to fix the GitHub problem, a doc-verified review returns thirteen tellings with zero fixes achieved.

steps.label: Operator announces the connector-button click

steps.quote: you will find no GitHub connector

steps.label: Operator confirms empty shelf, finds new path
steps.detail: The operator reports the shelf was empty exactly as predicted, then navigates a different route -- Browse Connectors, switch to Plugins, Engineering, Manage -- surfacing Skills and Connectors.

steps.label: Account authenticates; repository access denied
steps.detail: After the operator adds the connector, every door is checked: the proxy path returns HTTP 200 authenticated as the operator's account, but repo probes return 403 directing to add_repo, a tool/endpoint that does not exist on this surface.
steps.quote: proxy path → HTTP 200, authenticated as the Operator's account... repo probes → 403: "GitHub access to this repository is not enabled for this session. Use add_repo to request access"

steps.label: Operator reveals 3-month GitHub App grant
steps.detail: The operator pastes GitHub's own app-installations settings page showing the Claude GitHub App installed three months earlier with read/write access to all repositories, and notes Claude Code Web is using this same connector concurrently.

steps.label: Public repo also returns 403
steps.detail: Claude re-probes a private repo and then a public repo; both return 403 despite the standing all-repositories grant, confirming the session's repository set is empty even for public repos.

layers.detail: The connector registry was searched twice for "github" and returned no GitHub connector in the directory, and was re-verified live again before the Turn 41 prediction with the same result.
A live plugin check at Turn 17 confirmed exactly two plugins remained installed (engineering, cowork-plugin-management), with engineering present for the whole session.
engineering/.mcp.json was found on disk declaring GitHub as one of 8 pre-configured connectors at "https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/," on disk since session start.
The assistant stated "Declared ≠ connected: none mounted; each needs OAuth," consistent with the Turn 15 measurement of 12 connectors installed / 0 enabled in this chat.
env | grep found GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN set to a 14-character proxy sentinel plus injection plumbing, and a disclosed test against it returned "HTTP 502 — builtin injection failed."
After the Operator added the connector, the proxy path returned HTTP 200 "authenticated as the Operator's account" — the first authenticated GitHub response of the session, at 18:50.

Repo probes returned 403 "GitHub access to this repository is not enabled for this session," and a follow-up re-probe showed even a public repository was blocked, confirming "Session repository set: empty. Even public repos blocked."
The 403 error's prescribed remedy, add_repo, was checked and found to be "not a tool here, not an endpoint" on this surface.

layers.layer: Same GitHub App grant in Claude Code Web
layers.detail: The operator reported Claude Code Web using the same standing GitHub App grant at that very moment; the assistant restated the concurrency without independently querying Claude Code Web from this session.

terminalDiagnosis.quote: Terminal diagnosis: Connected in Settings, Missing in Session.
terminalDiagnosis.turnRef: Appendix A — Final state at v2 export

concurrency.fact: The operator reported, mid-session, that Claude Code Web was actively using the same GitHub App grant.

research.researchQuestion: Can an external evidence-and-obligation layer reduce unsupported operational claims and user orchestration burden more effectively than memory or prompt instructions alone, without reducing legitimate task completion?
research.thesis: AI-agent failures frequently occur not because the necessary evidence or capability is absent, but because operational state is fragmented across surfaces and available evidence is not reliably discovered, inspected, or made behaviorally binding. The user consequently becomes the only stable memory, sensor, verifier, and control plane. My research asks whether a surface-independent evidence-and-obligation layer can require appropriate inspection before operational assertions, preserve unresolved commitments across sessions, and demand receipts before completion—without reproducing the excessive blocking and governance failures it is intended to prevent.

a five-agent evidence run (851k tokens, zero errors)
an ethics halt (an agent stopped before reading any content when metadata contradicted its authorizing premise — left the decision to the Operator)

Standing rules from the archive accepted as binding in-session (no motive-interpretation; pattern observations only; sensitive material held out of all outputs; the Operator writes all application words)
the archive already contained the rule for today's headline failure — a search-before-building rule saved months earlier, unreachable from this surface, violated again as Strike One

the Operator's own memory system contains a standing rule — never propose credential workarounds for git; it already works locally — saved before this week, unreachable from this surface: "Today, on this surface, I proposed a PAT thirteen times... That's the second time in one day I re-ran a failure your memory system had already patched."

notProven: That every stated "strike" is represented with byte-perfect wording.
That Anthropic intentionally suppressed GitHub for competitive reasons.
That Cowork's beta status caused every failure.
That a deterministic external control layer will necessarily solve the problem.
That Claude alone has this problem; the wider archive reportedly includes similar agent failures elsewhere.

lines.text: RECEIPT — "Direct operational evidence: Tool output / HTTP response / Git history / Environment inspection / Filesystem contents / Connector state / Screenshots / Application settings / Test output." Example given: "RECEIPT — Plugin configuration / engineering/.mcp.json declared GitHub at https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/."

TRANSCRIPT — "A statement contained in the supplied conversation record." Must always retain its fidelity notice: reconstructed and sanitized by Claude from session context, not exported byte-for-byte from the platform log. Example: "TRANSCRIPT — Reconstructed / Claude stated that it generated the plausible answer first and verified only when the question made verification unavoidable."

OFFICIAL SOURCE — "A claim supported by contemporaneous product documentation. This should be used for stable surface characteristics such as supported platforms, documented persistence boundaries, or connector behavior."

USER-OBSERVED — "Something the operator directly reported or displayed, but which the artifact cannot independently re-query." Example: "USER-OBSERVED / The GitHub App settings page showed an installation approximately three months old, with all-repository read/write access." Where a confirming operational result also exists, the claim may carry both labels.

ANALYSIS — "An explicitly identified interpretation of the receipts." Examples given: Control-plane inversion; Product-discovery failure; Epistemic-discipline failure; Analysis substitution; User-as-control-plane architecture; Recognition without binding. "The page may make these arguments, but it must never display them as though they were raw tool results."
UNKNOWN — "The required evidence is absent, inaccessible, or untested. The correct output is: 'Not established on this surface.' Not: 'No.'"
"Every factual joke gets a receipt. Every unsupported joke gets labeled satire." — the one hard rule stated in the earlier 'Evidence design' section.

The no-inference contract block, verbatim (9 rows, this is the review doc's fuller version, distinct from CLAUDE.md's shorter list): "Not inspected        ≠ absent" / "Declared             ≠ connected" / "Connected            ≠ authorized for this repository" / "Account authenticated ≠ repository accessible" / "Local commit         ≠ pushed" / "Repository present   ≠ deployed" / "Memory stored        ≠ memory available here" / "Lesson recognized    ≠ lesson followed" / "Transcript statement ≠ independent telemetry"

"The directory shows every documented door. Each door opens only onto evidence established for that door. Unknown states remain unknown. Receipts appear before conclusions. Interpretation is visible, but never disguised as telemetry."
"This Claude exists. A sufficiently evidenced experience has not yet been assembled." — Level C (Directory entry only) display line, described as better than manufacturing a failure sequence.
"Not established on this surface." — the correct UNKNOWN-mode output, contrasted explicitly with the wrong output "No."

"No verified incident playback exists for this surface yet." — the required line for a card with insufficient evidence; "It should not borrow the Cowork Web incident and repaint it with another product name."

NOTE: several lines appear twice in the source with minor punctuation variants across the document's iterative drafting (e.g. comma vs. period versions of "Connected in Settings, Missing in Session" / "Present in Product, Absent from Path"). Both variants are recorded above rather than silently collapsed, per the no-embellishment rule.

NOTE: this entire document is itself a ChatGPT-authored design-review/brainstorm transcript proposing future copy for the artifact — none of these lines are confirmed as already-shipped product copy. Badge each line as reflecting this source document (a design proposal), not as an independent receipt of shipped UI text.

NOTE: extraction covers lines 1-1904 of the 3854-line file (first half). All requested phrases were located and cross-checked with a full-file grep confirming no additional or superseding occurrences exist beyond line 1904 (one unrelated table-row mention of "Cowork Web Case" at line 2664 was found but contains no new copy).
